title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description:
You are given an unsorted list of integers `nums`.

Return the length of the longest run of consecutive integers that can be formed using values from `nums` (each value used at most once, run must be strictly increasing by 1, values need not be adjacent in the input). Aim for O(n) time — sorting first would cost O(n log n).

**Example**

```
nums = [100, 4, 200, 1, 3, 2]  ->  4   (the run 1, 2, 3, 4)
```
hints: Put everything in a set first so membership checks are O(1).
Only start counting a run from a number x where x - 1 is NOT in the set — that guarantees x is the start of its run, so each run gets counted exactly once.
From each valid start, keep checking x+1, x+2, ... in the set to measure the run length.

solutions.name: Hash set, count from run starts only
solutions.explanation: Load all values into a set for O(1) lookups. For each value x, only begin extending a run if x - 1 is absent from the set — this means x is the smallest element of its run, so each run is measured starting exactly once (never re-walked from the middle). From a valid start, keep incrementing while the next consecutive value is present, tracking the longest length seen.
